rationale: Each ensures-forall is duplicated for the post1_self_ and post2_self_ runs but with disjoint per-run triggers, so z3 never fires both at the same skolem. The prelude introduces fused witnesses (assert forall|x| ... post1_self_.X(x) =~= post2_self_.X(x)) and, inside each by-body, asserts the matching post2-domain-membership so the second copy of the ensures-forall instantiates at the same x; the shared pre_self_ then chains post1.X(x) = pre.X(x) = post2.X(x) for every field pinned by containers_tree_unchanged / processes_fields_unchanged / threads_unchanged and for the parent-children, subtree_set, and uppertree_seq foralls.
